title: Adyen Checkout API
overview: Adyen Checkout API v71. A single multi-method payment entry point: POST /payments starts a transaction for any of 40+ payment methods, distinguished by the paymentMethod.type discriminator (a oneOf union over per-method detail objects). POST /payments/details continues redirect/3DS flows; POST /paymentMethods lists the methods available to a shopper. The same path therefore serves many payment products.

security_schemes.name: ApiKeyAuth
security_schemes.details: API key sent in the X-API-Key request header.

endpoints.summary: Get a list of available payment methods

schemas.name: Amount

fields.name: currency
fields.description: The three-character [ISO currency code](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/currency-codes#currency-codes) of the amount.

fields.name: value
fields.description: The numeric value of the amount, in [minor units](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/currency-codes#minor-units).

schemas.constraints: currency: ISO 4217 three-letter code; value: minor units (integer).

schemas.name: PaymentRequest

fields.name: amount
fields.description: The amount information for the transaction (Amount object).

fields.name: merchantAccount
fields.description: The merchant account identifier, with which you want to process the transaction.

fields.name: reference
fields.description: The reference to uniquely identify a payment. This reference is used in all communication with you about the payment status. To provide multiple references for one transaction, separate the reference values with the hyphen (`-`) characte...

fields.name: returnUrl
fields.description: The URL to return to in case of a redirection. The format depends on the channel. * For web, include the protocol `http://` or `https://`. You can also include your own additional query parameters, for example, shopper ID or order refere...

fields.name: paymentMethod
fields.description: The type and required details of a payment method, selected by the `type` discriminator (schemeâ†’CardDetails, idealâ†’IdealDetails, applepayâ†’ApplePayDetails, ...). The full spec lists 40+ members; three representative ones are captured here as named schemas.

fields.name: channel
fields.description: The platform where a payment transaction takes place. This field is optional for filtering out payment methods that are only available on specific platforms. If this value is not set, then we will try to infer it from the `sdkVersion` or...

fields.name: countryCode
fields.description: The shopper country code. Format: [ISO 3166-1 alpha-2](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1_alpha-2) Example: NL or DE

fields.name: shopperReference
fields.description: Required for recurring payments. Your reference to uniquely identify this shopper, for example user ID or account ID. Minimum length: 3 characters. > Your reference must not include personally identifiable information (PII), for example ...

schemas.constraints: paymentMethod is a oneOf union discriminated by paymentMethod.type.

schemas.name: CardDetails

fields.name: type
fields.description: Default payment method details. Common for scheme payment methods, and for simple payment method details.

fields.name: number
fields.description: The card number. Only collect raw card data if you are [fully PCI compliant](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/pci-dss-compliance-guide).

fields.name: expiryMonth
fields.description: The card expiry month. Only collect raw card data if you are [fully PCI compliant](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/pci-dss-compliance-guide).

fields.name: expiryYear
fields.description: The card expiry year. Only collect raw card data if you are [fully PCI compliant](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/pci-dss-compliance-guide).

fields.name: cvc
fields.description: The card verification code. Only collect raw card data if you are [fully PCI compliant](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/pci-dss-compliance-guide).

fields.name: holderName
fields.description: The name of the card holder.

fields.name: encryptedCardNumber
fields.description: The encrypted card number.

fields.name: encryptedExpiryMonth
fields.description: The encrypted card expiry month.

fields.name: encryptedExpiryYear
fields.description: The encrypted card expiry year.

fields.name: encryptedSecurityCode
fields.description: The encrypted card verification code.

fields.name: brand
fields.description: Secondary brand of the card. For example: **plastix**, **hmclub**.

fields.name: storedPaymentMethodId
fields.description: This is the `recurringDetailReference` returned in the response when you created the token.

schemas.constraints: type discriminator default 'scheme'. Use raw number/expiry/cvc (card-direct) OR the encrypted* fields (client-side encryption); the spec does not state the encryption algorithm.

fields.name: type
fields.description: **ideal**

fields.name: issuer
fields.description: The iDEAL issuer value of the shopper's selected bank. Set this to an **id** of an iDEAL issuer to preselect it.

fields.name: storedPaymentMethodId
fields.description: This is the `recurringDetailReference` returned in the response when you created the token.

schemas.name: ApplePayDetails

fields.name: type
fields.description: **applepay**

fields.name: applePayToken
fields.description: The stringified and base64 encoded `paymentData` you retrieved from the Apple framework.

fields.name: fundingSource
fields.description: The funding source that should be used when multiple sources are available. For Brazilian combo cards, by default the funding source is credit. To use debit, set this value to **debit**.

schemas.constraints: type discriminator default 'applepay'; applePayToken is required.

schemas.name: PaymentResponse

fields.name: resultCode
fields.description: The result of the payment. For more information, see [Result codes](https://docs.adyen.com/online-payments/payment-result-codes). Possible values: * **AuthenticationFinished** â€“ The payment has been successfully authenticated with 3D Sec...

fields.name: pspReference
fields.description: Adyen's 16-character string reference associated with the transaction/request. This value is globally unique; quote it when communicating with us about this request. > For payment methods that require a redirect or additional action, you...

fields.name: action
fields.description: Action to be taken for completing the payment.

fields.name: refusalReason
fields.description: If the payment's authorisation is refused or an error occurs during authorisation, this field holds Adyen's mapped reason for the refusal or a description of the error. When a transaction fails, the authorisation response includes `resul...

fields.name: refusalReasonCode
fields.description: Code that specifies the refusal reason. For more information, see [Authorisation refusal reasons](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/refusal-reasons).

fields.name: merchantReference
fields.description: The reference to uniquely identify a payment. This reference is used in all communication with you about the payment status. We recommend using a unique value per payment; however, it is not a requirement. If you need to provide multiple...

fields.name: amount
fields.description: Authorised amount in the transaction.

schemas.constraints: resultCode drives the next step (Authorised / RedirectShopper / ChallengeShopper / Refused / Pending ...). When not final, 'action' tells the client what to do next.

schemas.name: PaymentDetailsRequest

fields.name: details
fields.description: Use this collection to submit the details that were returned as a result of the `/payments` call.

fields.name: paymentData
fields.description: Encoded payment data. For [authorizing a payment after using 3D Secure 2 Authentication-only](https://docs.adyen.com/online-payments/3d-secure/other-3ds-flows/authentication-only/#authorise-the-payment-with-adyen): If you received `resul...

fields.name: threeDSAuthenticationOnly
fields.description: Change the `authenticationOnly` indicator originally set in the `/payments` request. Only needs to be set if you want to modify the value set previously.

schemas.constraints: details: key-value pairs returned by the redirect/3DS step.

fields.name: merchantAccount
fields.description: The merchant account identifier, with which you want to process the transaction.

fields.name: amount
fields.description: The amount information for the transaction (in [minor units](https://docs.adyen.com/development-resources/currency-codes)). For [BIN or card verification](https://docs.adyen.com/payment-methods/cards/bin-data-and-card-verification) reque...

fields.name: countryCode
fields.description: The shopper's country code.

fields.name: channel
fields.description: The platform where a payment transaction takes place. This field can be used for filtering out payment methods that are only available on specific platforms. Possible values: * iOS * Android * Web

fields.name: shopperReference
fields.description: Required for recurring payments. Your reference to uniquely identify this shopper, for example user ID or account ID. The value is case-sensitive and must be at least three characters. > Your reference must not include personally identif...

fields.name: allowedPaymentMethods
fields.description: List of payment methods to be presented to the shopper. To refer to payment methods, use their [payment method type](https://docs.adyen.com/payment-methods/payment-method-types). Example: `"allowedPaymentMethods":["ideal","applepay"]`

fields.name: blockedPaymentMethods
fields.description: List of payment methods to be hidden from the shopper. To refer to payment methods, use their [payment method type](https://docs.adyen.com/payment-methods/payment-method-types). Example: `"blockedPaymentMethods":["ideal","applepay"]`

schemas.name: PaymentMethodsResponse

fields.name: paymentMethods
fields.description: Detailed list of payment methods required to generate payment forms.

fields.name: storedPaymentMethods
fields.description: List of all stored payment methods.

schemas.constraints: paymentMethods: methods available for the given amount/country/shopper.

fields.name: status
fields.description: The HTTP response status.

fields.name: errorCode
fields.description: The error code mapped to the error message.

fields.name: message
fields.description: A short explanation of the issue.

fields.name: errorType
fields.description: The category of the error.

fields.name: pspReference
fields.description: The PSP reference of the payment.

operational.detail: POST /payments serves all payment methods; the paymentMethod.type discriminator selects which detail object (and therefore which required fields) apply.
When resultCode is RedirectShopper/ChallengeShopper, the client follows 'action' then calls POST /payments/details with the returned details.

missing: Client-side encryption (CSE) algorithm for the encrypted* card fields is not stated in this spec.
Webhook/notification payloads and HMAC signing live in a separate Adyen Notification API, not in the Checkout spec â€” no callbacks captured here.
